VoicePage.title: AI Voice Triage
VoicePage.subtitle: Speak your symptoms in your local language. SahiDawa AI will help you understand next steps.

VoicePage.processing_subtitle: Checking your symptoms with SahiDawa AI…
VoicePage.review_message: The microphone picked up your symptoms with low confidence. You can retry for a clearer result or analyse this transcript anyway.
VoicePage.emergency_body: These symptoms may need urgent care. Call 112 or 108 right away, or go to the nearest emergency facility.
VoicePage.disclaimer: This guidance is for informational use only and is not a diagnosis. Consult a doctor or pharmacist, especially for severe or worsening symptoms.
VoicePage.stop_listening_aria: Listening for symptoms. Tap to stop.
VoicePage.footer_note: Voice guidance is informative only. For serious symptoms, please consult a licensed doctor immediately.

VoicePage.fallback_summary: SahiDawa could not structure the response clearly. Please consult a doctor if your symptoms continue.

errors.timeout_message: The voice service is taking longer than expected. Please try again or switch to text input.
errors.api_message: We could not get medical guidance right now. Please try again in a moment.

contact.footer: SahiDawa is free, open-source, and built for 1.4 billion Indians. No ads. No premium. No data sold. Ever.

about.heroSubtitle: India's first open-source citizen medicine verifier & rural health bridge. Built for Bharat. Not just India.

problemSection.title: The Problem We're Solving
problemSection.description: India has a three-layer healthcare crisis that no existing platform solves simultaneously.

fakeMedicines.title: Fake Medicines
fakeMedicines.description: 12–25% of medicines in India are fake or substandard — putting 1.4 billion people at risk with zero citizen-facing verification tool.

ruralHealthcare.title: Rural Healthcare Gap
ruralHealthcare.description: 65% of India's population lives in rural areas with almost no qualified doctors — over 900 million people without accessible healthcare.

languageBarrier.title: Language Barrier
languageBarrier.description: 22 official languages — but health information is mostly in English or Hindi, leaving 500M+ non-Hindi speakers behind.

realIncident.title: Real Incident — July 2025
realIncident.text: Delhi Police busted a counterfeit medicine ring supplying fake Johnson & Johnson and GSK medicines — made of chalk powder and starch — all the way into government hospitals. Patients had <highlight>zero way to verify</highlight> these medicines before consuming them.
realIncident.highlight: SahiDawa fixes this. For free. Forever. Open source.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To empower every Indian citizen — regardless of language, location, or literacy — with the ability to instantly verify medicines, access qualified health guidance, and report counterfeit drugs in their community.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A Bharat where no child dies from a fake medicine, no farmer's family is misdiagnosed for lack of a doctor, and no language is a barrier to healthcare. Free. Open. Forever.

contactUs.title: Get In Touch
contactUs.subtitle: Have questions, ideas, or want to contribute? We'd love to hear from you.

Faq.title: Frequently Asked
Faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about SahiDawa — India's free, open-source medicine verification platform.

cta.title: Still have questions?
cta.subtitle: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Reach out to our community.

0.question: What is SahiDawa?
0.answer: SahiDawa is India's first open-source citizen medicine verification platform. It helps you verify if your medicine is real or fake, find safe pharmacies nearby, and get health guidance in your language.

1.question: Is SahiDawa free to use?
1.answer: Yes! SahiDawa is 100% free forever. No ads, no premium plan, no data sold. Ever. It is built for Bharat, by the community.

2.question: How does medicine verification work?
2.answer: You can scan the barcode or QR code on your medicine packaging. SahiDawa checks it against the official CDSCO database and tells you if the medicine is real, suspicious, or fake.

3.question: Which languages does SahiDawa support?
3.answer: SahiDawa supports 22 official Indian languages. You can speak your symptoms in your own language using our Voice Health Assistant powered by Whisper and Sarvam AI.

4.question: How can I report a fake medicine?
4.answer: You can use the 'Report Fake' feature on the homepage. Your report gets added to a community heatmap that helps alert others in your district about counterfeit medicines.

5.question: Is my data safe with SahiDawa?
5.answer: Absolutely. SahiDawa does not sell your data, show ads, or have any premium plan. Your privacy is our top priority.

6.question: How can I contribute to SahiDawa?
6.answer: SahiDawa is open source under MIT License. You can contribute on GitHub, report bugs, suggest features, or join as a GSSoC 2026 participant.

7.question: What is CDSCO?
7.answer: CDSCO stands for Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation. It is India's national regulatory body for cosmetics, pharmaceuticals and medical devices. SahiDawa verifies medicines against their official database.
